title: Aristolochia laheyana
taxonomy: Aristolochia laheyana is a species of flowering plant in the Aristolochiaceae family. This genus is part of the larger order Piperales. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is classified as an evergreen perennial. Further classification of Aristolochia laheyana may include its genus and species within the broader classification of angiosperms.

intro: Aristolochia laheyana, also known as Lahey's Dutchman's Pipe, is a species of flowering plant in the Aristolochiaceae family, characterized by its unique, curved flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Aristolochia laheyana include aristolochic acids, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aristolochia laheyana is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
